scenario: A marketplace running Camunda 8.8 keeps 75 workers split across several services. The shared starter configuration still sets camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.max-jobs-active for global capacity caps; the team must move every property under the documented replacement subtree before removing the legacy block from application.yaml entirely.
question: Which property replaces the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.max-jobs-active in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Set camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active as the global cap on jobs activated per worker at a time
Set camunda.client.worker.override.max-jobs-active as the global cap on jobs activated per worker at a time
Set camunda.client.cloud.defaults.max-jobs-active as the global cap on jobs activated per worker at a time
Set camunda.client.defaults.max-jobs-active as the global cap on jobs activated per worker at a time

a.text: Correct. The reference table lists camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.max-jobs-active property in Camunda 8.8 starter configuration.

b.text: Incorrect. The override subtree is keyed by job type for per-worker overrides; the global defaults block keeps the capacity cap and replaces the zeebe.defaults form.

c.text: Incorrect. The cloud subtree holds SaaS cluster identifiers such as cluster-id and region; worker capacity caps move under camunda.client.worker.defaults.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no camunda.client.defaults subtree documented in the reference; the documented replacement lives under camunda.client.worker.defaults.

explanation: The zeebe.defaults block in the starter reference is deprecated and one-to-one replaced by the worker.defaults block. Every property under camunda.client.zeebe.defaults has a sibling under camunda.client.worker.defaults that carries the same value semantics in Camunda 8.8.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
